title: Network Defenses

headings.text: Stateful Inspection Firewalls (Layer 3 & 4 – Network & Transport Layers)
Application-Level Gateways / Proxy Firewalls (Layer 7 – Application Layer)
Switches, Routers, and Network Infrastructure Logs

overview: Network Intrusion Detection System (NIDS) is either a piece of software or a dedicated appliance that inspects network traffic and raises alerts when suspicious or malicious behavior is detected. These alerts are passed on to security analysts for investigation.

study.overview: Network Intrusion Detection System (NIDS) is either a piece of software or a dedicated appliance that inspects network traffic and raises alerts when suspicious or malicious behavior is detected. These alerts are passed on to security analysts for investigation.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: Stateful Inspection Firewalls (Layer 3 & 4 – Network & Transport Layers)
Application-Level Gateways / Proxy Firewalls (Layer 7 – Application Layer)
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
tips: By collecting and correlating all these logs, analysts can detect early warning signs of attacks, such as scanning, data exfiltration, or lateral movement, and respond before major damage occurs.
